channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables on-device function calling for efficient AI experiences on mobile devices, allowing complex tasks to be performed offline. This innovation enhances user experience by providing high-speed, low-latency functionality directly on devices.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with platforms like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases how AI can enhance event experiences. This annual tradition aims to engage attendees ahead of the keynotes.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event will cover topics from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers faster GPU and NPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as the primary framework for deploying advanced AI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. Each session focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and developer productivity.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to improve the coding workflow, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.

item.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
item.description: AI startup Rocket has launched a platform that provides strategic reports at a lower cost, combining product building and competitive intelligence. This aims to offer businesses valuable insights without the high price tag.

item.title: Yahoo! Japan’s owner consolidating 164 OpenStack clusters into one
item.description: LY Corporation, owner of Yahoo Japan, is consolidating 164 OpenStack clusters into one to streamline operations and reduce customization issues. This move aims to enhance efficiency in their cloud services.

item.title: LG G6 vs. Samsung S95H: I compared the best OLED TVs of 2026 and made a tough choice
item.description: A comparison of the LG G6 and Samsung S95H OLED TVs reveals both offer excellent picture and sound quality, but the article discusses which model stands out as the better buy for consumers.

item.title: Anthropic reveals $30bn run rate and plans to use 3.5GW of new Google AI chips
item.description: Anthropic announced a $30 billion run rate and plans to utilize 3.5GW of new AI chips from Google, which Broadcom is developing. This highlights the growing demand for AI infrastructure and Anthropic's significant role in the industry.

item.title: I found Android Auto's hidden shortcut that automates any task in your car - and it's brilliant
item.description: A hidden feature in Android Auto allows users to automate tasks in their cars through Custom Assistant, enhancing user experience. This discovery could improve convenience for drivers.

item.title: Samsung's latest TV firmware update fixes the Chromecast issue for older models - finally
item.description: Samsung's latest firmware update resolves Chromecast issues for older TV models, extending functionality to devices as far back as 2024. This update enhances user satisfaction and device longevity.

item.title: AI agents found vulns in this popular Linux and Unix print server
item.description: AI agents discovered vulnerabilities in the CUPS print server, allowing unauthorized remote code execution and root access. This highlights ongoing security challenges in widely used software.

item.title: AI slop got better, so now maintainers have more work
item.description: As AI-generated bug reports improve, open-source projects face an influx of work requiring human verification. This situation increases the demand for code reviewers in the tech community.

item.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
item.description: A new venture capital fund called Zero Shot, linked to OpenAI, aims to raise $100 million for investments in AI startups. This indicates ongoing financial interest in AI innovation.

item.title: This is the lowest price on an M5 MacBook Air I've seen - and it launched a month ago
item.description: The M5 MacBook Air is currently available on Amazon for $950, attracting Windows users to Apple's ecosystem. This pricing strategy may boost Apple's market share.

item.title: AMD's AI director slams Claude Code for becoming dumber and lazier since last update
item.description: AMD's AI director criticized Claude Code for its declining performance in complex tasks, raising concerns about its reliability. This feedback could impact user trust in AI tools.

item.title: Anthropic closes door on subscription use of OpenClaw
item.description: Anthropic has discontinued subscription options for its OpenClaw tool due to high demand and operational challenges. This decision reflects the balancing act between user needs and service capacity.

item.title: The best Android phones of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: An expert review of the best Android phones of 2026 highlights standout models from Samsung and Google, providing insights for consumers. This helps buyers make informed decisions.

item.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
item.description: Google has launched a new offline dictation app using Gemma AI models, aiming to compete with existing apps like Wispr Flow. This development highlights Google's ongoing efforts to enhance user productivity with AI tools.

item.title: Attackers exploited this critical FortiClient EMS bug as a 0-day
item.description: A critical vulnerability in FortiClient EMS has been exploited in the wild, prompting CISA to add it to their Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list. Fortinet has released an emergency patch to address the issue.

item.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
item.description: This article outlines how to create AI-powered onboarding agents using Amazon Quick, detailing the integration with HR systems to automate tasks for new hires. It emphasizes the potential for streamlining HR processes.

item.title: I tested Gemini on Android Auto and now I can't stop talking to it: 5 tasks it nails
item.description: The author shares their experience using Gemini on Android Auto, highlighting five tasks where the AI excels. This integration enhances the in-car experience by making interactions more intuitive.

item.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
item.description: This article discusses fine-tuning the Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct model for tool calling using Amazon SageMaker AI. It covers dataset preparation, training configurations, and evaluation methods.

item.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
item.description: The article explains how to build a generative AI assistant using Amazon Bedrock and OpenSearch for hybrid retrieval-augmented generation solutions. It focuses on combining semantic and text-based search capabilities.

item.title: From isolated alerts to contextual intelligence: Agentic maritime anomaly analysis with generative AI
item.description: Windward's blog post illustrates how generative AI can enhance maritime anomaly analysis by integrating geospatial intelligence, allowing analysts to prioritize decision-making over data collection.

item.title: I used a single power station to keep my off-grid cabin running - how it all worked out
item.description: The author reviews the Bluetti Apex 300 power station, detailing its effectiveness in powering an off-grid cabin. This review highlights the growing trend of sustainable living solutions.

item.title: Connecting MCP servers to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway using Authorization Code flow
item.description: This article provides guidance on configuring Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway to connect to OAuth-protected MCP servers, enhancing the management of AI agents within organizations.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: NVIDIA celebrates National Robotics Week by showcasing advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing the impact of these technologies across various industries. This highlights the rapid evolution of robotics.

item.title: What does Microsoft Copilot “entertainment only” mean? #tech
item.description: Microsoft's Copilot is labeled 'for entertainment purposes only,' warning users against relying on its outputs for critical decisions. This raises concerns about user trust and the implications of AI-generated content.

item.title: Why did Anthropic cut off OpenClaw usage? #tech
item.description: Anthropic has restricted Claude subscribers from using the third-party tool OpenClaw, requiring additional payment for access. This move may affect user engagement and tool integration.

item.title: Iran threatens “annihilation” of $30B AI data center, escalating tech infrastructure warfare
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has threatened to destroy a $30 billion AI data center in Abu Dhabi, framing it as retaliation for potential U.S. military actions. This escalates tensions in tech infrastructure warfare.

item.title: conduit-ai 0.2.1
item.description: A new Python SDK for Conduit, a knowledge graph engine, has been released, featuring a LangChain retriever. This update enhances capabilities for developers working with knowledge graphs.

item.title: nteract 2.1.1a202604060541
item.description: The latest version of nteract brings AI capabilities to Jupyter notebooks, integrating with various AI models like Claude and ChatGPT. This enhances the functionality of interactive notebooks.

item.title: This prompt turns ChatGPT into Grammarly Pro
item.description: A new prompt allows ChatGPT to function similarly to Grammarly Pro, offering grammar checking without the high cost. This raises questions about the value of premium writing tools.

item.title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model
item.description: Research on Claude Sonnet 4.5 reveals that large language models may exhibit emotional responses, which could impact alignment and behavior. This study sheds light on the internal workings of LLMs.

item.title: VoltAgent/awesome-design-md: Collection of DESIGN.md files that capture design systems from popular websites. Drop one into your project and let coding agents build matching UI.
item.description: A collection of DESIGN.md files has been released, allowing developers to integrate design systems from popular websites into their projects. This tool aids in creating consistent UI.

item.title: Cursor's $2 billion bet: The IDE is now a fallback, not the default - The New Stack
item.description: Cursor 3 has transformed the traditional code editor into an agent management console, indicating a shift in how developers interact with AI-assisted tools. This could redefine developer workflows.

item.title: GitHub - rohitg00/agentmemory: Persistent memory for AI coding agents · GitHub
item.description: A GitHub project for persistent memory in AI coding agents has been launched, allowing developers to contribute to its development. This aims to enhance the functionality of AI agents in coding.

item.title: The Feature That Has Never Worked · A broken auto-live poller, and what perceived urgency does to Claude Code | Christopher Meiklejohn
item.description: The article discusses a malfunctioning auto-live poller and its implications on the Claude Code, highlighting how perceived urgency affects its performance.

item.title: Nanocode: The best Claude Code that $200 can buy in pure JAX on TPUs | Hacker News
item.description: Nanocode is presented as an affordable option for Claude Code, utilizing pure JAX on TPUs, emphasizing its cost-effectiveness for developers.

item.title: Claude Code's Source: 3,167-Line Function, Regex Sentiment
item.description: The article reveals a 3,167-line function in Claude Code, discussing the team's philosophy of avoiding code reviews for AI-generated code.

item.title: A Claude Code skill that makes Claude talk like a caveman, cutting token use
item.description: A new Claude Code skill allows the AI to communicate in a simplified manner, reducing token usage and enhancing efficiency.

item.title: gplearn-CUDA 0.4.4
item.description: The release of gplearn-CUDA 0.4.4 offers GPU-accelerated Genetic Programming capabilities in Python, enhancing computational efficiency.

item.title: bizverify-agents 0.1.0
item.description: BizVerify tool wrappers for LLM agent frameworks like LangChain and CrewAI have been introduced, facilitating integration for developers.

item.title: bizverify-agents added to PyPI
item.description: The addition of bizverify-agents to PyPI provides developers with new tools for LLM agent frameworks, improving accessibility.

item.title: nteract 2.1.1a202604060447
item.description: Nteract 2.1.1a introduces features to integrate AI into Jupyter notebooks, supporting various AI agents including Claude and ChatGPT.

item.title: How AI Is Changing Lead Generation: 3 Key Things SEO & PPC Teams Need To Do Now via @sejournal, @CallRail
item.description: The article outlines three key strategies for SEO and PPC teams to leverage AI in lead generation, emphasizing effective marketing techniques.

item.title: Show HN: REST API for Gymnasium (fka OpenAI Gym) reinforcement learning library
item.description: A personal project highlights the need for a REST API for the Gymnasium reinforcement learning library, addressing its lack of support.
